$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; attribute schema, supports, usesContext/providesContext carried verbatim for parity.

Delegates to the registered core/template-part edit (forked-block cutover keeps it registered) so the fork inherits the upstream + V1 editor surface and the same @wordpress/core-data shim selectors. Re-porting was rejected to avoid regressing the V1 nav-editor / template-part editing surfaces (#413 acceptance criterion).
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced server-side from stamped _resolved* attributes.
Bidirectional core/template-part ↔ artisanpack/template-part block transforms for the rollout window.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx delegates to the registered core/template-part edit rather than re-porting upstream edit.js — chosen to inherit the V1 editor surface unchanged and read entity data through the same core-data shim selectors.
template-part is server-rendered: the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers from stamped _resolved* attributes, not by save().
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that do not exist upstream — required to coexist with core/template-part during the V1 rollout.
